name: Google Gemini Live API
pricing_notes: Gemini 2.0 Flash Live: $0.70/M audio input tokens, $3.00/M output; 15 RPM free tier

auth.notes: GOOGLE_API_KEY; also supports Vertex AI credentials for enterprise

performance.notes: Bidirectional WebSocket streaming; designed for sub-600ms voice response latency

agent_readiness.notes: Bidirectional audio/video streaming with Gemini 2.0 Flash. Real-time audio input + output. Screen sharing capture. Tool/function calling mid-stream. Native Google Search grounding. Audio-native (not STT+LLM+TTS pipeline).

gotchas: WebSocket-based API requires stateful connection management
15 RPM free tier very restrictive for development
Function calling mid-stream is powerful but complex to orchestrate
best_when: Real-time voice AI applications needing audio-native (no STT/TTS stitching)
Applications leveraging Google Search grounding for live data
Low-latency voice agents where stitched pipelines add too much latency
avoid_when: Production apps requiring stable API (currently beta)
Cost-sensitive voice workloads (per-token pricing adds up)
summary: Google's Gemini Live API enables real-time bidirectional audio/video streaming with Gemini 2.0 Flash. Audio-native architecture (not stitched STT/LLM/TTS) means lower latency than pipeline approaches. Supports tool calling mid-stream, Google Search grounding, and screen capture. Currently in beta with rate limit restrictions, but technically impressive for real-time multimodal agents.
